titleStmt.title: SII 16.26: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 26. (A.R. No. 255 of 1905.) TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab set up to the east of the Chīkaṭimidde of the Tripurāntakēśvara temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1345, Śōbhakṛit, expressed by the chronogram bāṇa-yugaanala-indu, Pausha ba. 10, Monday when the sun was entering Mṛigarāśi. The corresponding date would be 1423 A.D., December 27, Monday, °73, if Mṛigarāśi stands for Makara-rāśi.

This inscription in Sanskrit verse, states that when Vijaya Bukka, son of Dēvarāya was ruling over the kingdom from Vijayanagara, his minister Nāgayāmātya of Hārīta-gōtra, son of Chauṇḍapa and Śrīmāyī, having worshipped God Tripurān- taka and the goddes Tripurāṁbika at Śrīśaila, bought a rice-field worth 300 nishkas from Kumāra Bhikshāvṛitti and granted the same to the god. Nāgayāmātya is described as the foremost among the Upapradhānas. Mentions Gaṅgāṁba as the donor's wife and Liṅganārya, son of Gaṅgādharāchārya, as the composer of the record.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
dēvaḥśrītripurāṁbāyā baṁdhurgaṁdhavatītaṭē . kumāraśailanila
yaḥ pāyādva stripurāṁtakaḥ . Astibhūlōkadēvēṁdrō dēvarāyō ma
hīpatiḥ . vijaya stasya putrōbhūt bhūpālakulaśēkharaḥ . rājā
dhirāja paramēśvara vīrabukka bhūpētha śāsati mahīṁ mahanīya
kīrttau . Āsētugautami manōjñaguṇābhirāmā madhyāsya mūla
nagarīṁ vijayābhidhānāṁ . tasya maṁtriśirōratnaṁ guṇaratnamahōdadhiḥ .
gōtrarājasamō dhairyyē hārītamunigōtrajaḥ . śrīmāyīgarbhasaṁ
bhūtaḥ cauṁḍapāmātyanaṁdanaḥ . nāgayāmātyavaryyōsti dvitīyya Iva nāgarāṭ . gaṁ
gāṁbbānāma tasyāsīddēvī śaṁbhōrivāṁbikā . lakṣmīrvviśvaṁbharasyēva bhāratīvābja janmanaḥ . sa sa
mētya mahāmaṁtrī śrīśailē tripurāṁtakaṁ . samabhyarcya mahādēvaṁ dēvīṁ�ca tripurā
ṁbikāṁ . śākē bāṇayugānalēṁdugaṇitē śrīśōbhakṛdvatsarē pauṣē māsi sitētarē ca
daśamē ghasrē himāṁśōrddinē . saptāśvē mṛgarāśi saṁkramamitē puṇyē ma
hāparvaṇi prādānnāgayamaṁtriśēkharamaṇiḥ kṣētraṁ purārātayē .
dēvyā śrītripurāṁbāyā dēvasya tripuradviṣaḥ . nityōpahāravidhayē kē
saridrōṇasaṁmitāṁ . gaṁdhavatyāstaṭē ramyē śālēyaphalaśālinīṁ . śatatrayē
ṇa niṣkāṇāṁ mūlyēna vyevahārataḥ . krītvā kumārabhikṣāvṛttīśvarā dyatiśēkharāt . datvā
bhuvaṁ mahābhaktyā babhūva sacivāgraṇīḥ . śrī nāgayāmātyavarēṇa dattāṁ bhuṁjan
mahīṁ śrītripurāṁttakēśaḥ . Upapradhānāgrasarāya tasmai dadā tyabhīṣṭārddhaphalābhi
vṛddhiṁ . purōdhasā śrītripurāṁtakasya gaṁgādharācāryyavarātmajēna . śrī
liṁgaṇāryyēṇa vinirmmitōyaṁ jīyācciraṁ śāsanapadyabaṁdhaḥ . liṁgayavi
duṣā racitaṁ nāgaya maṁtrīṁdra dharmmaśāsanamidaṁ . Āru
dramāsamudraṁ bhūyādācaṁdramāmahēṁdraṁ ca .
sāmānyōyaṁ dharmmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanī
yō bhavadbhiḥ . Itthaṁ sarvvān bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēṁdrān bhūyō
bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ . maṁgaḷamahāśrīśrīśrī .
svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ .
paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niḥṣphalaṁ bhavēt .
Digital edition of SII 16.26 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
